decision_summary.rationale: Implementing a lifecycle policy to transition objects older than 90 days to Glacier Instant Retrieval will significantly reduce storage costs for this archive bucket without impacting data availability for infrequent access.

llm_response.technical_explanation: The S3 bucket 'company-data-archive-prod' currently stores all its objects in S3 Standard, incurring higher costs. Analysis shows that 84.7% of the objects are older than 90 days, indicating they are cold data suitable for archival storage. By implementing an S3 lifecycle policy, objects will automatically transition to Glacier Instant Retrieval (GLACIER_IR) after 90 days, reducing the monthly storage cost from $42.38 to $8.29, resulting in monthly savings of $34.09. Glacier Instant Retrieval offers millisecond retrieval times, making it suitable for data that needs to be accessed infrequently but quickly when required.

risks: Potential for increased retrieval costs if data is accessed frequently after transition.
risk_assessment.verification_steps: Confirm that data in 'company-data-archive-prod' is indeed infrequently accessed and that the retrieval costs associated with Glacier Instant Retrieval are acceptable for the expected access patterns.

nl_quality.name: nl_quality
nl_quality.details: The report covers core metrics (84.7%, $34.09 savings) with consistent numbers and good resource specificity, but omits the total object count (84,320), misses the requirement to transition noncurrent versions, and doesn't mention the aws_s3_bucket_lifecycle_configuration separate-resource constraint.
